input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

task_context.question: What is the setting for the game engine Snowdrop?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: What is the setting for the game engine Snowdrop?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
First-hop evidence: Snowdrop (game engine) — "for use on Microsoft Windows, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One."  
Bridge needed: Confirm that Snowdrop officially supports the listed platforms (Microsoft Windows; Nintendo Switch; PlayStation 4; Xbox One).  
Retrieval direction: 1. Fetch the page titled "Snowdrop (game engine)" (Wikipedia) and inspect the lead and infobox for 'Platforms'/'Supported platforms'. 2. Fetch Ubisoft's official Snowdrop engine page (or search "Snowdrop game engine site:ubisoft.com") and check 'Supported platforms' or technical/specs sections.

current_output.query:
1) Fetch the Wikipedia page:
GET https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Snowdrop_(game_engine)
- Extract the lead paragraph and the infobox "Platforms" / "Supported platforms" field (copy exact text).

2) Fetch Ubisoft's official Snowdrop information:
a) Try direct Ubisoft engine URL:
GET https://www.ubisoft.com/en-us/engine/snowdrop
b) If (a) not available, try:
GET https://snowdrop.ubisoft.com
c) If direct pages fail, run a site-restricted search:
Search query: site:ubisoft.com "Snowdrop" "engine" "platform" OR "supported"
- From any Ubisoft page returned, extract the supported platforms list or technical/spec statement and copy the exact text/snippet and URL.

3) If either source lists platforms, compare against the list: Microsoft Windows; Nintendo Switch; PlayStation 4; Xbox One. Return the confirmed list and cite the source(s) and the exact text/snippets used for confirmation.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Snowdrop (game engine) | Snowdrop is a proprietary game engine created by Ubisoft for use on Microsoft Windows, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One. It was first revealed at E3 2013 with "Tom Clancy's The Division", the first game using the engine.

Ubisoft Motion Tracking Camera | The Ubisoft Motion Tracking Camera is a USB camera developed by Ubisoft, that was bundled with the 2009 exercise video game "Your Shape" for Microsoft Windows and Wii. Developed at Ubisoft Barcelona, the device adds full-body motion tracking to the platforms, to allow the "Your Shape" game to detect the player's movements. The camera is also supported by Ubisoft's "Racquet Sports" (2010) and "Fit in Six" (2011). Variations of these games for PlayStation 3 or Xbox ...[truncated]

Rayman Legends | Rayman Legends is a platform video game developed by Ubisoft Montpellier and published by Ubisoft. It is the fifth main title in the "Rayman" series and the direct sequel to the 2011 game "Rayman Origins". The game was released for Microsoft Windows, Xbox 360, PlayStation 3, Wii U, and PlayStation Vita platforms in August and September 2013. PlayStation 4 and Xbox One versions were released in February 2014. An exclusive Nintendo Switch port, titled Rayman Legends Definitive Edi ...[truncated]

Child of Light | Child of Light is a platforming role-playing video game developed by Ubisoft Montreal and published by Ubisoft for Microsoft Windows, the PlayStation 3, PlayStation 4, Wii U, Xbox 360 and Xbox One in April 2014, and was released on PlayStation Vita in July 2014. It is powered by UbiArt Framework.

Trials Fusion | Trials Fusion is a platform racing video game developed by RedLynx in collaboration with Ubisoft Shanghai and Ubisoft Kiev. A follow-up to the critically acclaimed "Trials Evolution", it is the fifth game in the "Trials" series and is the first one to be released on a PlayStation platform. The game was released on Microsoft Windows, PlayStation 4, Xbox 360 and Xbox One in April 2014. A companion game, "Trials Frontier", released on iOS and Android devices and is designed to accom ...[truncated]

Prince of Persia (2008 video game) | Prince of Persia is an action-adventure and platforming video game developed by Ubisoft Montreal and published by Ubisoft. It is part of the "Prince of Persia" franchise. The game was released in the United States on December 2, 2008 for PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360 and on December 9, 2008 for Microsoft Windows. It was later released on March 24, 2009 for Mac OS X via the Cider engine.

Grow Up (video game) | Grow Up is an open world adventure platform video game developed by Ubisoft Reflections and published by Ubisoft. The game, which is the sequel to 2015's "Grow Home", was released on 16 August 2016 for Microsoft Windows, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One.
source_state.summary_2: Snowdrop is a proprietary game engine created by Ubisoft "for use on Microsoft Windows, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One." (Snowdrop (game engine))
source_state.answer: Microsoft Windows, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One
